introduction: Area measures the region enclosed by a plane figure. Competitive exams test standard formulas, unit conversions, and composite shapes built from rectangles, triangles, and circles.
explanation:
CONCEPT EXPLANATION

Rectangle: A = l × b. Square: A = a².

Triangle: A = ½ × base × height.

Parallelogram: A = base × height.

Trapezium: A = ½ × (sum of parallel sides) × height.

Circle: A = πr². Semicircle: ½πr².

Rhombus: A = ½ × d₁ × d₂.

Composite figures: split into basic shapes, find each area, add or subtract.

Always keep units consistent (cm, m, etc.).

REAL-LIFE APPLICATIONS

Land measurement, flooring tiles, painting walls, agricultural plots, and material estimation in construction.

COMMON MISTAKES

1) Using slant side instead of perpendicular height in triangles/trapeziums.
2) Forgetting to square the radius in πr².
3) Mixing diameter and radius.
4) Wrong unit conversion (cm² to m² divides by 10000).
5) Not subtracting cut-out regions in composite figures.

IMPORTANT NOTES

• π ≈ 22/7 or 3.14 as specified.
• 1 m² = 10,000 cm².
• Equilateral triangle side a: A = (√3/4)a².
• Heron's formula: A = √[s(s−a)(s−b)(s−c)] where s = semi-perimeter.

shortcutTips.title: Radius vs diameter
shortcutTips.tip: Check whether the given value is r or d before using πr².

shortcutTips.title: Height perpendicular
shortcutTips.tip: Height must be perpendicular to the base, not the slant edge.

shortcutTips.title: Composite split
shortcutTips.tip: Divide irregular shapes into rectangles and triangles.

shortcutTips.title: Unit squaring
shortcutTips.tip: Converting cm to m? Square the conversion factor too.

shortcutTips.title: Semicircle
shortcutTips.tip: Area is half the circle; don't confuse with arc length.

shortcutTips.title: Trapezium average base
shortcutTips.tip: Average of parallel sides × height gives the same result.

solvedExamples.problem: Triangle base 10 cm, height 6 cm. Area?
Trapezium parallel sides 8 cm and 12 cm, height 5 cm. Area?
